methodology: Cross-reference network (JOS.5.13‚ÜíGEN.18.2 = 9v strongest in entire study, EXO.3.2 = 66 refs hub) + patristic evidence (13 citations: Eusebius christophanic identification, Didymus mediating position, Gregory of Nyssa 'pele=divine incomprehensibility', Hippolytus pre-incarnate Son) + CCEL commentaries (JFB 'Jehovah-Jesus' systematic chain, Henry 'eternal Word', Wesley, Calvin 'agree with ancients', Barnes) + topics (ANGEL HOLY TRINITY = 25 refs classifying as 'ONE OF THE HOLY TRINITY') + dictionary (Easton 'foreshadowings of incarnation', Smith 'Christ's visible form before incarnation')

evidence: CCEL (Henry 'God himself, the eternal Word, angel of the covenant', Wesley identical)

new_refs_added.justification: FIRST appearance of Angel of the LORD in the Bible. Gn 16:10 has 75 refs (most connected). Links to 5/8 gold refs. Hagar names him 'El Roi' (God who sees) in 16:13 ‚Äî narrative confirmation of divine identity. Didymus: 'God dwelt in him.' Calvin: 'Christ the Mediator present in all oracles.' Topic ANGEL(HOLY TRINITY) includes Gn 16:7,9.

7 patristic comments! Eusebius: 'no other than he who spoke to Moses' ‚Äî most explicit christophanic identification. ACCEPTS WORSHIP (contrast Rev 19:10 where created angel REFUSES). v.15 = EXACT echo of Ex 3:5 ('remove sandals'). 9v‚ÜíGEN.18.2 (strongest connection in ENTIRE study). JFB: 'angel of the covenant.'

STRONGEST identity claim of any appearance: Angel claims YHWH's historical acts in 1st person ('I brought you up from Egypt, I said I will never break my covenant'). No created angel could claim the Exodus. 3v‚ÜêJDG.13.3.

candidates_noted.justification: Key theological text: 'my Name is in him' ‚Äî in Semitic thought, God's Name IS God's presence. 4v‚ÜêEXO.3.2. JFB cites in complete identification chain.
'Angel of his PRESENCE' (mal'ak panav) ‚Äî theological synthesis of the entire tradition. Cross-ref in multiple gold files (v=2-3). Topic ANGEL(HOLY TRINITY) includes.
Balaam episode ‚Äî only case where Angel acts as ADVERSARY (satan). 23 refs. Haydock identifies as Michael. Topic ANGEL(HOLY TRINITY) includes.

scholarly_notes.identity_switch_phenomenon: In nearly EVERY appearance, text begins calling figure 'Angel' then switches to 'LORD' or 'God' speaking in 1st person. Patterns: (A) Narrator switches referents (Ex 3:2‚Üí3:4‚Üí3:6 = 3 terms in 4 verses), (B) Angel speaks in divine 1st person (Gn 22:12, 16:10, Jdg 2:1), (C) Humans respond as if seeing GOD (Gn 16:13, Jdg 6:22, 13:22), (D) Distinction within unity (Zec 3:2 speaks AS YHWH but references YHWH in 3rd person). No other angel (Gabriel, Michael) ever exhibits this pattern.

three_theological_positions.christophany: Eusebius, Hippolytus, Henry, Wesley, JFB, Barnes, Calvin (with nuance). Henry: 'prelude to what he intended in the fulness of time.' JFB: 'ALL OT manifestations in the person of the Divine Son.'
three_theological_positions.created_angel_ambassador: Haydock, Challoner, John Damascene, Augustine (partially). Principle of agency: ambassador speaks in sender's 1st person.
three_theological_positions.mediating: Calvin, Didymus, Jerome (partially). Didymus: 'God DWELT in him.' Jerome: typical significance points to Christ even if literal figure is created angel.

scholarly_notes.strongest_connection: JOS.5.13‚ÜíGEN.18.2 = 9 votes ‚Äî strongest individual connection in the entire study. Links Commander of LORD's army to Abraham's three visitors: two theophanies with 'drawn sword.'
